title: Rich Crispy German Squid Fritters
description: Crispy, nutty fritters made with fresh squid, spiced batter, and a sweet mustard glaze that warms the German kitchen.
instructions: In a mixing bowl, combine flour, baking powder, salt, and pepper; stir until evenly mixed.
Whisk the egg and beer together until frothy, then gradually whisk into the dry mixture to form a smooth batter; let rest 10 minutes.
Stir in the German mustard, sugar, and chopped dill until fully incorporated.
Gently fold the cleaned squid rings into the batter, ensuring they are well coated.
Heat the oil in a deep skillet to 180°C (350°F) over medium-high heat.
Drop spoonfuls of the battered squid into the hot oil, frying 2-3 minutes per side until golden brown and crispy.
Use a slotted spoon to transfer fritters to a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
In a small saucepan, simmer the remaining mustard glaze over low heat, stirring until it thickens slightly.
Drizzle the glaze over the freshly fried fritters and sprinkle with lemon zest.
Serve immediately with a side of German rye bread or a light salad.
